title: atriplex cinerea subsp. bolusii
intro: Atriplex cinerea subsp. bolusii, also known as Bolus's orache, is a subspecies of the Atriplex cinerea plant, a type of succulent that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions, with its leaves and stems having anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Bolus's orache include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Bolus's orache can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used as a poultice for skin conditions.
